package agent

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"regexp"
	"strings"
	"sync"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/evidence"
)

// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
	Log          io.Writer
	EventSink    io.Writer
	ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
	PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
	// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, is invoked between nextCandidate and
	// Claim. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute against the
	// loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed routing
	// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
	// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
	// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
	// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
	// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only
	// consumes the typed-incompatibility surface.
	RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
	Once           bool
	PollInterval   time.Duration
	NoReview       bool
	LabelFilter    string
	SessionID      string
	WorkerID       string
	ProjectRoot    string
	// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
	// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
	// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid a poll-interval-sized delay
	// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
	// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
	// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
	WakeCh <-chan struct{}
}

// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3

// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour

// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
// advance the base branch.
const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute

// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
// worker.
const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute

// handleOutcomeStoreError is called when a Store.* operation in the
// post-Execute outcome block returns a non-nil error. It logs the error,
// appends a kind:loop-error event to the bead (best-effort), sets a short
// cooldown so the bead is not immediately re-queued, increments
// result.Failures, and returns true — signalling the caller to continue to
// the next loop iteration rather than returning.
//
// If ctx is already cancelled, it returns false so the caller can propagate
// the cancellation immediately.
func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx context.Context, beadID, operation string, storeErr error, assignee string, result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, now func() time.Time) bool {
	if ctx.Err() != nil {
		return false
	}
	if runtime.Log != nil {
		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", operation, beadID, storeErr)
	}
	_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
		Kind:      "loop-error",
		Summary:   operation + " failed",
		Body:      storeErr.Error(),

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os/exec"
7		"strings"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// NoChangesContract — TD-031 §8.1 (ddx-b24e9630). When the agent returns
12	// no_changes the loop now gates the close on a structured marker in
13	// no_changes_rationale.txt. The marker is one of:
14	//
15	//   - verification_command: <shell command>
16	//     (the bead is closed as already_satisfied iff the command exits 0)
17	//   - status: needs_investigation
18	//     reason: <free-form text>
19	//     (the bead stays open with a needs-investigation label and event)
20	//
21	// A rationale carrying neither marker is rejected: bead stays open with a
22	// no-changes-unjustified label + event. Status enum stays in the canonical
23	// bd/br 6 (open / in_progress / closed / blocked / proposed / cancelled);
24	// triage state lives in labels and events.
25	
26	// NoChangesRationaleKind classifies a parsed no_changes_rationale.txt body.
27	type NoChangesRationaleKind string
28	
29	const (
30		// NoChangesKindVerified — the rationale carries a verification_command
31		// the loop can run to prove the AC is currently met.
32		NoChangesKindVerified NoChangesRationaleKind = "verified"
33		// NoChangesKindNeedsInvestigation — the agent declared it cannot make
34		// progress and explained why; the bead must be triaged before retry.
35		NoChangesKindNeedsInvestigation NoChangesRationaleKind = "needs_investigation"
36		// NoChangesKindUnjustified — neither marker is present; the rationale is
37		// rejected and the bead stays open for triage.
38		NoChangesKindUnjustified NoChangesRationaleKind = "unjustified"
39	)
40	
41	// NoChangesContract event kinds (TD-031 §4 vocabulary; emitted on the bead's
42	// event stream by the execute-loop).
43	const (
44		NoChangesEventVerified           = "no_changes_verified"
45		NoChangesEventUnverified         = "no_changes_unverified"
46		NoChangesEventUnjustified        = "no_changes_unjustified"
47		NoChangesEventNeedsInvestigation = "no_changes_needs_investigation"
48	)
49	
50	// NoChangesContract triage labels (consumed by auto-triage, ddx-3c154349).
51	const (
52		NoChangesLabelUnverified         = "triage:no-changes-unverified"
53		NoChangesLabelUnjustified        = "triage:no-changes-unjustified"
54		NoChangesLabelNeedsInvestigation = "triage:needs-investigation"
55	)
56	
57	// ParsedNoChangesRationale is the structured form of a no_changes_rationale
58	// body. Only the marker(s) the agent included are populated.
59	type ParsedNoChangesRationale struct {
60		Kind                     NoChangesRationaleKind
61		VerificationCommand      string
62		NeedsInvestigationReason string
63	}
64	
65	// ParseNoChangesRationale extracts the NoChangesContract markers from a
66	// rationale body. verification_command takes precedence over needs_investigation
67	// when both are present (a verified rationale is the strongest signal).
68	func ParseNoChangesRationale(text string) ParsedNoChangesRationale {
69		p := ParsedNoChangesRationale{Kind: NoChangesKindUnjustified}
70		needsInv := false
71		var reasonLines []string
72		inReason := false
73		for _, raw := range strings.Split(text, "\n") {
74			line := strings.TrimSpace(raw)
75			lower := strings.ToLower(line)
76			switch {
77			case strings.HasPrefix(lower, "verification_command:"):
78				cmd := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("verification_command:"):])
79				if cmd != "" {
80					p.VerificationCommand = cmd
81					p.Kind = NoChangesKindVerified
82				}
83				inReason = false
84			case strings.HasPrefix(lower, "status:"):
85				v := strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(line[len("status:"):]))
86				if v == "needs_investigation" {
87					needsInv = true
88				}
89				inReason = false
90			case strings.HasPrefix(lower, "reason:"):
91				r := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("reason:"):])
92				if r != "" {
93					reasonLines = append(reasonLines, r)
94				}
95				inReason = true
96			default:
97				if inReason && line != "" {
98					reasonLines = append(reasonLines, line)
99				}
100			}
101		}
102		if p.Kind == NoChangesKindVerified {
103			return p
104		}
105		if needsInv {
106			p.Kind = NoChangesKindNeedsInvestigation
107			p.NeedsInvestigationReason = strings.Join(reasonLines, " ")
108		}
109		return p
110	}
111	
112	// VerificationCommandRunner runs a shell verification command in projectRoot.
113	// A non-zero exit code is reported via exitCode (NOT err); err is reserved for
114	// failures to launch (binary missing, timeout). Output is the combined
115	// stdout+stderr capped by the implementation.
116	type VerificationCommandRunner func(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, command string) (exitCode int, output string, err error)
117	
118	// DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout bounds how long the loop will wait for a
119	// verification_command to finish before treating it as failed.
120	const DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout = 60 * time.Second
121	
122	// DefaultVerificationCommandRunner runs `command` via /bin/sh -c in projectRoot
123	// with DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout.
124	func DefaultVerificationCommandRunner(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, command string) (int, string, error) {
125		cctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout)
126		defer cancel()
127		cmd := exec.CommandContext(cctx, "/bin/sh", "-c", command)
128		if projectRoot != "" {
129			cmd.Dir = projectRoot
130		}
131		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
132		if cctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded {
133			return -1, truncateVerifyOutput(string(out)), fmt.Errorf("verification_command timed out after %s", DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout)
134		}
135		if exitErr, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
136			return exitErr.ExitCode(), truncateVerifyOutput(string(out)), nil
137		}
138		if err != nil {
139			return -1, truncateVerifyOutput(string(out)), err
140		}
141		return 0, truncateVerifyOutput(string(out)), nil
142	}
143	
144	// truncateVerifyOutput caps verification command output before it is recorded
145	// on the bead's event stream so a chatty test suite cannot blow up the event
146	// body.
147	func truncateVerifyOutput(s string) string {
148		const max = 4 * 1024
149		if len(s) <= max {
150			return s
151		}
152		return s[:max] + "\n…[truncated]"
153	}
154

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"testing"
6	
7		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
8	)
9	
10	func TestParseNoChangesRationale(t *testing.T) {
11		cases := []struct {
12			name string
13			in   string
14			want ParsedNoChangesRationale
15		}{
16			{
17				name: "empty",
18				in:   "",
19				want: ParsedNoChangesRationale{Kind: NoChangesKindUnjustified},
20			},
21			{
22				name: "bare",
23				in:   "nothing to do here",
24				want: ParsedNoChangesRationale{Kind: NoChangesKindUnjustified},
25			},
26			{
27				name: "verification_command basic",
28				in:   "verification_command: go test ./foo -run TestX\noutput: PASS",
29				want: ParsedNoChangesRationale{Kind: NoChangesKindVerified, VerificationCommand: "go test ./foo -run TestX"},
30			},
31			{
32				name: "needs_investigation with reason",
33				in:   "status: needs_investigation\nreason: provider quota unknown\nmore detail line",
34				want: ParsedNoChangesRationale{Kind: NoChangesKindNeedsInvestigation, NeedsInvestigationReason: "provider quota unknown more detail line"},
35			},
36			{
37				name: "verification_command takes precedence over needs_investigation",
38				in:   "status: needs_investigation\nverification_command: true",
39				want: ParsedNoChangesRationale{Kind: NoChangesKindVerified, VerificationCommand: "true"},
40			},
41			{
42				name: "case insensitive markers",
43				in:   "Verification_Command: ls\noutput: x",
44				want: ParsedNoChangesRationale{Kind: NoChangesKindVerified, VerificationCommand: "ls"},
45			},
46		}
47		for _, tc := range cases {
48			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
49				got := ParseNoChangesRationale(tc.in)
50				assert.Equal(t, tc.want, got)
51			})
52		}
53	}
54	
55	func TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunner(t *testing.T) {
56		t.Run("exit 0", func(t *testing.T) {
57			code, _, err := DefaultVerificationCommandRunner(context.Background(), "", "true")
58			assert.NoError(t, err)
59			assert.Equal(t, 0, code)
60		})
61		t.Run("exit non-zero", func(t *testing.T) {
62			code, _, err := DefaultVerificationCommandRunner(context.Background(), "", "false")
63			assert.NoError(t, err)
64			assert.NotEqual(t, 0, code)
65		})
66	}
67

b543f084 fix(agent): gate no_changes close on NoChangesContract markers [ddx-b24e9630]
---
commit b543f08488d5b62129e694111bc1ca8704131346
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Sat May 2 15:51:50 2026 -0400

    fix(agent): gate no_changes close on NoChangesContract markers [ddx-b24e9630]
    
    Distinguish "AC verified, nothing to do" from "agent gave up without
    progress". When execute-bead returns no_changes the loop now parses
    no_changes_rationale.txt for one of two markers (TD-031 §8.1
    NoChangesContract):
    
      - verification_command: <shell command> — loop runs the command in
        project root (60s timeout). Exit 0 → bead closed as already_satisfied
        with a no_changes_verified event. Non-zero → triage:no-changes-unverified
        label + no_changes_unverified event, bead stays open.
      - status: needs_investigation + reason: <why> — triage:needs-investigation
        label + no_changes_needs_investigation event, bead stays open.
    
    Bare rationales (no marker) are rejected: triage:no-changes-unjustified
    label + no_changes_unjustified event, bead stays open. The legacy
    rationaleIsSpecific / count-based heuristic is no longer the default
    adjudication path; SatisfactionChecker remains as an explicit escape
    valve. Status enum stays in the bd/br canonical 6 — all triage state
    lives in labels and events (ADR-004).
    
    Execute-bead prompt instructions updated to require one of the markers
    in any no_changes_rationale.txt the agent writes.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

verification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/ -run "TestParseNoChangesRationale|TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunner|TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesUnjustifiedStaysOpenWithLabel|TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerifiedClosesImmediately|TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerificationFailsKeepsBeadOpen|TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesNeedsInvestigationKeepsBeadOpen" -count=1 && go test ./internal/bead/ -run SchemaCompat -count=1

status: already_satisfied

The NoChangesContract (TD-031 §8.1, ddx-b24e9630) is already fully implemented
in HEAD via commit b543f084 ("fix(agent): gate no_changes close on
NoChangesContract markers [ddx-b24e9630]"), which is in this worktree's
ancestry through the pre-execute-bead checkpoint at 081f9682.

Evidence by AC:

1. Prompt requires marker — cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go lines 1322-1325
   ("If you write `{{.AttemptDir}}/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one
   of: `verification_command: ...` ... `status: needs_investigation` + `reason:
   ...`. Bare rationales (\"nothing to do\") are rejected.").

2. Loop verifies verification_command before closing — cli/internal/agent/
   execute_bead_loop.go:1622 calls ParseNoChangesRationale; the
   NoChangesKindVerified branch (lines 1624-1641) runs the command via
   VerificationCommandRunner and only closes on exit 0; non-zero falls through
   to the unverified label/event path.

3. Labels not new statuses — cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_no_changes_verify.go
   defines triage:no-changes-unverified, triage:no-changes-unjustified, and
   triage:needs-investigation. Status stays in the bd/br canonical 6
   (open/in_progress/closed/blocked/proposed/cancelled). bd/br round-trip via
   `ddx bead export` is unchanged because the bead schema gained no new status
   value — labels are already a first-class field.

4. Canonical event names — execute_bead_no_changes_verify.go defines
   no_changes_verified, no_changes_unverified, no_changes_unjustified, and
   no_changes_needs_investigation; emitted from execute_bead_loop.go:1639-1653.

5. Tests cover all four paths — execute_bead_loop_test.go:
     - verified→close: TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerifiedClosesImmediately (line 992)
     - unverified→stay-open+label: TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerificationFailsKeepsBeadOpen (line 1052)
     - unjustified→stay-open+label: TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesUnjustifiedStaysOpenWithLabel (line 730)
     - needs-investigation→stay-open+label: TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesNeedsInvestigationKeepsBeadOpen (line 1098)
   Plus parser-level coverage in execute_bead_no_changes_verify_test.go.

6. Existing closed beads not retroactively reopened — the new gate only fires
   on the post-Execute outcome path for beads the loop has just executed; no
   migration or retroactive scan touches already-closed beads.

7. Auto-triage consumes the labels — wiring landed in commit e188d9d3
   ("feat(agent): wire triage dispatch for lock_contention/execution_failed/
   no_changes [ddx-3c154349]"); the triage:* labels defined here are the
   contract surface for that consumer.

8. schema_compat — internal/bead/schema_compat_test.go passes (no new status
   values were added).

Verified locally: `go test ./internal/agent/ -run "NoChanges|Triage" -count=1`
and `go test ./internal/bead/ -run SchemaCompat -count=1` both pass.

Per CLAUDE.md (YAGNI / DOWITYTD), no further changes are warranted: the
contract, prompt update, loop gate, events, labels, and tests are all in
place at HEAD.
